description_long: This is a large, ancient-style ceramic krater vase with a wide, open mouth and a deep, rounded body. The exterior is predominantly black with a reddish-brown base and rim, featuring classical Greek red-figure artwork depicting human figures in draped garments. Two robust, horizontal handles are attached on opposite sides, each with a decorative circular motif at the base. The rim is thick and slightly flared, showing some visible wear and surface cracks, suggesting age or use. The interior is dark and appears unfinished, consistent with traditional ceramic vessels. The overall form and decoration evoke the look of an artifact suited for display in a public or ceremonial urban setting.
description: A large, black and reddish-brown ceramic krater vase with Greek red-figure artwork and two sturdy side handles. The vase features a wide mouth, flared rim, and visible surface wear.
description_view_0: The front view highlights the detailed red-figure artwork of standing human figures and the wide, open mouth of the vase.
description_view_1: This side view emphasizes one of the thick, rounded handles and the decorative motif at its base, with less artwork visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays both handles and a broader section of the red-figure artwork, showing multiple figures and the full curvature of the vase.
description_view_3: This side view focuses on the opposite handle, with visible surface cracks and a partial view of the artwork.
attribute_pattern: classical human figures and geometric borders
